L1.area: Protocol buffer message decoding and field processing

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the protobuf decoding logic where crafted untrusted input with deeply nested structures can cause unbounded stack recursion. On architectures with stack probes (e.g., x86), this leads to denial of service, while on architectures without stack probes (e.g., ARM), overflowing the stack is unsound and can result in memory corruption or potential remote code execution.

L3.area: Protocol buffer wire format field decoding and message deserialization
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the protobuf wire format decoding logic where crafted untrusted input with deeply nested group structures triggers unbounded stack recursion during deserialization. The decoder's handling of nested group wire types has no recursion depth limit, so a specially crafted message containing a large sequence of group-start markers can overflow the stack. On architectures with stack probes (e.g., x86), this leads to denial of service, while on architectures without stack probes (e.g., ARM), overflowing the stack results in memory corruption or potential remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The skip_field function recursively processes nested protobuf group wire types without any recursion depth limit, allowing crafted input to overflow the stack.
